prompt: Two-panel illustration of a wooden test-tube stand, on a dark surface. Panel (a): a test tube containing a uniform red blood sample (before centrifugation). Panel (b): the same test tube after centrifugation, now showing three separated layers — a yellowish straw-coloured plasma at the top, a thin pale buffy-coat layer of platelets and white blood cells in the middle, and a dense dark-red layer of red blood cells at the bottom. Each layer labelled. Soft side light. Label: (a) Whole blood, (b) Plasma, Platelets and white blood cells, Red blood cells, Test tube stand. Dark background, orange accent labels, clean editorial illustration style.
alt: Donate Blood — The Science Behind a Life-Saving Gift

prompt: Portrait illustration of Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis, Indian paediatrician (1934–2022). Warm, gentle expression, wearing a simple shirt or doctor's coat. Glasses, kind eyes. Neutral dark background. Clean editorial illustration style with subtle orange accent. No text.
alt: Meet the Scientist — Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis (1934–2022)

prompt: Four-panel illustration of natural crystals arranged in a 2x2 grid on a clean dark background. Top-left: a pile of pinkish-orange rock salt (Himalayan sendha namak) chunks with visible crystal faces. Top-right: clear, sparkling lumps of Indian rock sugar / mishri / candy sugar on a small white plate. Bottom-left: a single intricate six-armed snowflake at high magnification, with delicate symmetric branching. Bottom-right: macro photograph-style of feathery white frost crystals on a glass window pane in low morning light. Each panel labelled (a) Rock salt, (b) Mishri (candy sugar), (c) Snowflake, (d) Frost. Dark cinematic background, orange accent labels, clean illustration style.
alt: Four natural crystals — rock salt, mishri sugar candy, a snowflake, and frost on a window

prompt: Traditional Indian distillation set-up known as the Deg-Bhapka method. Three large copper pots (degs) sit on small earthen furnaces in a row. From each deg, a long bamboo pipe (chonga) curves downward into a smaller round receiver (bhapka) submerged in a water-cooled trough. Steam visibly rising from the degs and condensing inside the bhapkas. Background: an old workshop wall in Kannauj. Show arrows indicating direction of vapour travel from deg through bamboo pipe into bhapka. Label: Deg (heating pot), Bamboo pipe, Bhapka (receiver), Cooling water trough. Warm earthy tones, dark background, orange accent labels, clean editorial illustration style.
alt: Mitti ka Ittar — Bottling the Smell of First Rain

prompt: A clean lab separation setup showing sublimation-based purification. A china dish on a clay triangle on a tripod with a spirit lamp heating a mixture of white camphor powder and dark grey sand. An inverted funnel above the dish, plugged loosely with cotton at the narrow end. Pure white camphor crystals are visibly depositing on the inner surface of the inverted funnel as delicate needle-like crystals, while the dark sand remains in the dish. Labels: "Mixture (camphor + sand)", "Heat gently", "Pure camphor crystals deposit here", "Cotton plug". Dark background, clean illustration style, orange accent labels.
alt: Sublimation purification setup — camphor from sand

prompt: Paperfuge device diagram. A flat circular cardboard disc held vertically. A thin glass capillary tube containing a small red blood sample is taped horizontally across the disc, near its edge. Two long strings pass through two pinholes near the centre of the disc and extend symmetrically left and right; each string ends in a small wooden handle held by a hand. Show curved arrows indicating the disc spinning rapidly when the strings are pulled apart. Label: Cardboard disc, Blood sample, Tape, String, Handle. Dark background, orange accent labels, clean technical illustration style.
alt: Diagram of a paperfuge — a cardboard disc with a thin red blood-sample tube taped to it, looped through two long strings ending in wooden handles

prompt: A split photograph. Left: a large container of turbid, murky brown water labeled "Before coagulation — colloidal clay particles keep water cloudy". Right: the same container after adding alum (potassium alum / fitkari), showing a dramatic transformation — clear bright water on top with a thick layer of grey-brown floc settled at the bottom, labeled "After coagulation — charged particles neutralised, large floc settles". A hand dropping a small alum crystal into the water adds scale. Orange labels, dark background, educational chemistry photograph style.
alt: Coagulation in water treatment — before and after alum treatment

prompt: Three-panel illustration of the coagulation process, side-by-side, on a clean dark background. Panel (a) "Adding powdered alum to suspended impurities in water": A glass beaker of muddy brown water on a wooden tabletop, with a hand sprinkling white powdered alum into the top from a spoon. Panel (b) "Alum coagulates the suspended impurities": Same beaker, but the muddy particles are visibly clumping together into larger brown flakes throughout the water. Panel (c) "Impurities settle at the bottom": Same beaker, with the brown clumps now collected at the bottom, leaving clear water above. Show curved arrows between panels indicating progression. Label: (a) Powdered alum, Muddy water; (b) Coagulating particles; (c) Settled impurities. Dark background, orange accent labels, clean illustration style.
alt: Three-stage diagram showing muddy water treated with powdered alum: alum being sprinkled, particles clumping together, and settled clumps at the bottom of a beaker

prompt: A clean, professional decision-tree flowchart on a dark background. Top node (orange box): "What type of mixture is it?". Four branches flow downward: (1) "Solid + Liquid" → "Can solid be filtered?" → Yes: "Filtration" (green box); No: "Centrifugation". (2) "Two liquids" → "Different boiling points?" → Yes: "Distillation" (green box); No: "Chromatography or Extraction". (3) "Solid in solid" → "Does one sublime?" → Yes: "Sublimation"; No: "Based on colour? Chromatography". (4) "Colloid" → "Need to remove particles?" → "Coagulation then filtration". All terminal method boxes in bright green. Orange connecting lines and arrows. White text in boxes. Dark background, clean infographic design, educational style.
alt: Decision tree flowchart for choosing the right separation method

prompt: A clean portrait-format dark-background infographic showing three stacked industrial process flowcharts arranged vertically: Water Treatment (top), Petroleum Refining (middle), Drug Manufacturing (bottom). Each shows 3–4 icon steps connected by downward orange arrows. White icons, orange arrows, minimal labels. Portrait (3:4) format.

A Class 9 student (South Asian appearance, approximately 14 years old) standing at a clean school chemistry laboratory bench wearing complete PPE. Wearing: white lab coat, clear safety goggles with side shields, blue nitrile gloves, and closed-toe shoes. Hair neatly tied back. Holding a test tube carefully with proper technique. Behind them: neat rows of lab chemicals, a fume hood, safety shower sign. Bright clean lighting, friendly educational style. Orange labels pointing to each PPE item: "Safety Goggles", "Lab Coat (tucked and buttoned)", "Nitrile Gloves", "Closed-toe shoes", "Hair tied back". Positive, aspirational, professional.
alt: Student in full PPE in a school chemistry laboratory

prompt: A warm, respectful documentary-style split image. Left: an illustrated or stylised portrait of Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis — a gentle-looking elderly Indian man with kind eyes, wearing a white kurta, warm expression. Label: "Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis (1934–2022), Paediatrician, Bangladesh 1971". Right: a modern WHO-standard ORS sachet packet against a light background — blue and white packaging, "Oral Rehydration Salts ORS" clearly visible, with the WHO formula printed on it (2.6 g NaCl, 2.9 g Trisodium Citrate, 1.5 g KCl, 13.5 g Glucose per litre). Label: "The formula that saved 50+ million lives". Warm, dignified, educational.
alt: Dr. Dilip Mahalanabis and the ORS sachet

prompt: A stunning science-art composition. Main image: hyper-detailed artistic rendering of red blood cells (biconcave discs, bright red) floating through a translucent blue-tinted blood vessel, with scattered white blood cells and tiny platelets. In the foreground, a small sleek laboratory vial labeled "PFC-Based Oxygen Carrier — Artificial Blood Candidate" in orange text, with a slight blue iridescent shimmer to the liquid inside. Background: DNA helix and molecular structures softly glowing. Dark background, extremely high detail, cinematic, scientific beauty.
alt: Blood cells and artificial blood research vial

prompt: Educational hierarchical tree diagram on a deep black background (#050505). At the top: the word "MATTER" in clean white sans-serif. Two clean orange-amber (#F59E0B) branches descend to "PURE SUBSTANCES" and "MIXTURES" (white text). From "PURE SUBSTANCES", two short branches lead to small uniform-coloured tiles labelled "ELEMENT" and "COMPOUND". From "MIXTURES", two branches lead to "HOMOGENEOUS" (a uniformly-shaded copper-coloured tile) and "HETEROGENEOUS" (a granite-like tile with visible grains). From "HETEROGENEOUS", three further branches lead to three small beaker illustrations side by side: "SOLUTION" (a perfectly clear beaker with a few tiny dots), "COLLOID" (a milky beaker with a faint orange Tyndall light beam visible inside), "SUSPENSION" (a cloudy beaker with larger particles settling at the bottom). All branch lines and labels in warm orange-amber. Clean educational scientific style. No 3D, no glossy effects. Dark background with orange accents only.
alt: Hierarchical framework of the chapter: matter splits into pure substances and mixtures; mixtures split into homogeneous and heterogeneous; liquid heterogeneous mixtures split into three particle-size families — solutions, colloids, and suspensions.
